samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO using Dual-port RAM, Write controller, Read controller, read pointer sync, and write pointer sync.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <!-- Parameter Description -->
    <parameter_description>
      <state id="DEPTH">Depth of the FIFO (default value).</state>
      <state id="WIDTH">Width of the data bus (default value).</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <!-- Logic Description -->
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Declare the variables
        reg [DEPTH-1:0] wptr;
        reg [DEPTH-1:0] rptr;
        reg [DEPTH-1:0] wptr_buff;
        reg [DEPTH-1:0] rptr_buff;
        
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] waddr_bin;
        reg [WIDTH-1:0] raddr_bin;
        
        reg wen;
        reg ren;
        integer i;
        // Convert bin to gray
        function [DEPTH-1:0] bin_to_gray(input [DEPTH-1:0] bin);
            begin
                for(i=0;i<DEPTH-1;i=i+1)
                    bin[i] <= bin[i]^bin[i+1];
                bin[DEPTH-1] <= bin[DEPTH-1];
                bin_to_gray = bin;
            end
        endfunction
        
        // Convert gray to bin
        function [DEPTH-1:0] gray_to_bin(input [DEPTH-1:0] gray);
            begin
                gray_to_bin[DEPTH-1] = gray[DEPTH-1];
                for(i=DEPTH-2;i>=0;i=i-1)
                    gray_to_bin[i] = gray_to_bin[i+1]^gray[i];
            end
        endfunction
        
        
        // Implementing dual port ram as a submodule
        dual_port_RAM #(.depth(DEPTH), .width(WIDTH)) RAM(
            .wclk(wclk),
            .wenc(wen),
            .waddr(waddr_bin),
            .wdata(wdata),
            .rclk(rclk),
            .renc(ren),
            .raddr(raddr_bin),
            .rdata(rdata));
        
        
        // Write Controller
        // Write pointer update
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn)
            begin
                if(~wrstn)
                    wptr<=0;
                else if(winc & !wfull)
                    wptr <= wptr + 1;
            end
            
        // Write address update
        always @(wptr)
            begin
                waddr_bin <= gray_to_bin(wptr);
            end
            
        // Generate write enable
        always @(wptr,wptr_buff)
            begin
                wen <= ((wptr == {~rptr_buff[DEPTH-1],rptr_buff[DEPTH-2:0]}))? 0 : 1;
            end
            
            
        // Read Controller
        // Read pointer update
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn)
            begin
                if(~rrstn)
                    rptr <= 0;
                else if(rinc & !rempty)
                    rptr <= rptr + 1;
            end
                
                
        // Read address update
        always @(rptr)
            begin
                raddr_bin <= gray_to_bin(rptr);
            end
            
            
        // Generate read enable
        always @(rptr,rptr_buff)
            begin
                ren <= ((rptr == wptr_buff))? 0 : 1;
            end
            
            
        // Pointer Buffer
        // Write pointer sync buffer update
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn)
            begin
                if(~wrstn)
                    wptr_buff <= 0;
                else
                    wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
            
        // Read pointer sync buffer update
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn)
            begin
                if(~rrstn)
                    rptr_buff <= 0;
                else
                    rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        
        // FIFO full and empty
        // FIFO full condition
        assign wfull = ((({~rptr_buff[DEPTH-1],rptr_buff[DEPTH-2:0]}^wptr) == {(DEPTH){1'b1}})? 1 : 0);
        
        // FIFO empty condition
        assign rempty = ((wptr == rptr_syn)? 1 : 0);
    </logic_description>
    
    
    <implementation>
    Asynchronous FIFO with Dual-port RAM and Control Logic
    
    ## Overview
    This module implements an asynchronous FIFO, allowing data to be written and read simultaneously without contention.
    
    ## Design Details
    ### Dual-port RAM
    A dual-port RAM is instantiated to store data asynchronously. The RAM module handles write and read operations independently.
    
    #### Instantiation
    dual_port_RAM #(.depth(DEPTH), .width(WIDTH)) RAM(
        .wclk(wclk),
        .wenc(wen),
        .waddr(waddr_bin),
        .wdata(wdata),
        .rclk(rclk),
        .renc(ren),
        .raddr(raddr_bin),
        .rdata(rdata));
    ### Write Controller
    The write controller manages the write pointer and generates write enable signals.
    
    #### Write Pointer Update
    always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn)
        begin
            if(~wrstn)
                wptr<=0;
            else if(winc & !wfull)
                wptr <= wptr + 1;
        end
    #### Write Address Update
    always @(wptr)
        begin
            waddr_bin <= gray_to_bin(wptr);
        end
    #### Write Enable Generation
    always @(wptr,wptr_buff)
        begin
            wen <= ((wptr == {~rptr_buff[DEPTH-1],rptr_buff[DEPTH-2:0]}))? 0 : 1;
        end
    ### Read Controller
    The read controller manages the read pointer and generates read enable signals.
    
    #### Read Pointer Update
    always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn)
        begin
            if(~rrstn)
                rptr <= 0;
            else if(rinc & !rempty)
                rptr <= rptr + 1;
        end
    #### Read Address Update
    always @(rptr)
        begin
            raddr_bin <= gray_to_bin(rptr);
        end
    #### Read Enable Generation
    always @(rptr,rptr_buff)
        begin
            ren <= ((rptr == wptr_buff))? 0 : 1;
        end
    ### Pointer Buffer
    The pointer buffers synchronize read and write pointers across clock domains.
    
    #### Write Pointer Sync Buffer Update
    always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn)
        begin
            if(~wrstn)
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            else
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
        end
    #### Read Pointer Sync Buffer Update
    always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn)
        begin
            if(~rrstn)
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            else
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
        end
    ### FIFO Full and Empty Logic
    The full and empty flags indicate the status of the FIFO.
    
    #### FIFO Full Condition
    assign wfull = ((({~rptr_buff[DEPTH-1],rptr_buff[DEPTH-2:0]}^wptr) == {(DEPTH){1'b1}})? 1 : 0);
    #### FIFO Empty Condition
    assign rempty = ((wptr == rptr_syn)? 1 : 0);
    </implementation>
</module>
